name: gmail
description: Read and search {owner}'s Gmail and prepare DRAFT replies/emails for him. Use whenever {owner} wants to find or read an email or thread ("what did X email me", "find that thread about Y", "check my inbox"), or wants an email drafted ("draft a reply", "write an email to Z saying…"). Read + draft only: it searches/reads mail and writes to Drafts, but NEVER sends and never touches real (sent/received) mail — sending stays {owner}'s own action in Gmail.
instructions:
# Gmail — read & draft

Read and search {owner}'s Gmail, and prepare DRAFT emails for him. Backed by the Gmail
REST API using the same Google account as Calendar.

**Capability scope (hard limit): read + draft only.**
- Reading: `gmail_search` (Gmail query syntax) and `gmail_read_thread`.
- Drafting: `gmail_create_draft` (and via REST: list/update/delete-draft). These write
  ONLY to {owner}'s Drafts.
- This skill NEVER sends mail and NEVER touches real (sent/received) messages — no send,
  no archive, no label, no delete of inbox mail. Sending is always {owner}'s own action in Gmail.

## When to use
- "what did <person> email me", "find that thread about X", "check my inbox for Y" → search, then read the thread.
- "draft a reply to <thread>", "write an email to <person> saying…" → create a draft (never send).
- "tidy up / change that draft", "bin that draft" → update / delete a draft.

## How
- **Read:** `gmail_search` with a query like `from:jane newer_than:14d`, `subject:invoice has:attachment`,
  `is:unread label:work`. It returns stubs with `thread_id`; pass that to `gmail_read_thread` to read the
  whole conversation. Summarise it — do not paste raw email back to {owner}.
- **Draft:** `gmail_create_draft` with `to`, `subject`, `body`. For a REPLY, pass `thread_id` (from the thread
  you read) and `in_reply_to` set to that message's `Message-ID`, so Gmail threads it correctly.

## Reading etiquette
- Summarise: who, what they want, any deadline/ask. Quote only the lines that matter.
- It is {owner}'s private mail — never forward, paste wholesale, or repeat secrets, OTP/2FA codes,
  or passwords you happen to see.

## Drafting etiquette
- Write in {owner}'s voice: direct, concise, warm-but-efficient. Match the thread's register.
- Keep it tight; get to the ask. No filler, no AI throat-clearing.
- ALWAYS leave it as a draft and show {owner} the full text. Tell him it's saved to Drafts for him to send.
- Never imply you sent it, and never ask the system to send it — there is no send tool by design.

## Safety
- Only ever write to Drafts. If asked to "send", explain you've drafted it and he can send from Gmail
  (or that send isn't enabled for Alistair).
- Never delete or modify a real message; `delete-draft` removes a DRAFT only.

## If something fails
- **403 "insufficient authentication scopes"** → the Google token lacks Gmail scope. Re-mint it with
  `scripts/get_google_token.py` (uncomment the gmail.readonly + gmail.compose scopes) and update
  `GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN` in Railway.
- **Network error reaching Gmail** → allow `gmail.googleapis.com` and `oauth2.googleapis.com`.
- **Google not configured (503)** → set GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN / GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID / GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET.

sections.heading: When to use
sections.body:
- Read/search: "what did <person> email", "find that thread", "check my inbox for X".
- Draft: "draft a reply", "write an email to <person>" → DRAFT only, never sends.
- Manage drafts: update or delete an existing draft.

sections.heading: How
sections.body: Read with `gmail_search` (Gmail query syntax, e.g. `from:jane newer_than:14d`) then `gmail_read_thread` on the returned thread_id. Draft with `gmail_create_draft` (to/subject/body); for replies pass thread_id + in_reply_to (the original Message-ID) so it threads.

sections.heading: Reading etiquette
sections.body: Summarise who/what/deadline; quote only what matters. It's private mail — never forward wholesale or repeat secrets, OTP/2FA codes, or passwords.

sections.heading: Drafting etiquette
sections.body: Write in {owner}'s voice — direct, concise. Keep it tight. ALWAYS leave it as a draft and show him the full text; he sends it himself. Never imply it was sent.

sections.heading: Safety
sections.body: Read + draft only. Writes go to Drafts exclusively; never send, archive, label, or delete real mail. `delete-draft` removes a DRAFT only. If asked to send, say it's drafted for him to send from Gmail.

sections.heading: If something fails
sections.body:
- 403 insufficient scopes → re-mint the Google token with gmail.readonly + gmail.compose (scripts/get_google_token.py) and update GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN.
- Network → allow gmail.googleapis.com + oauth2.googleapis.com.
- 503 not configured → set the GOOGLE_* trio.
